title: Rank-preserving OOF ensemble with regime-specific weights
summary: Under shift, ensembles are often more reliable than single models, but isotonic calibration can change ROC-AUC by introducing ties.
rationale: Under shift, ensembles are often more reliable than single models, but isotonic calibration can change ROC-AUC by introducing ties. For this competition, preserving ordering matters more than perfectly calibrated probabilities.
implementation_hint: Blend orthogonal experts only from strict OOF predictions: current GBDT classifier, lap-ranker, survival expert, and CatBoost specialist. Optimize convex weights by regime (`dry slicks`, `wet/intermediate`, `Monaco 2025`, maybe early/mid/late race). Prefer rank averaging or sigmoid/temperature-style post-processing; avoid isotonic on the final submission blend unless it proves AUC-positive.
expected_effect: Usually the safest way to harvest small gains from diverse objectives without overcommitting to one inductive bias.
risk: If OOF generation is not perfectly clean, the blend will look better locally than on Kaggle; regime slicing can overfit when some subsets are small.
